breakout_products.title: 🚨 BREAKING: A TypeScript legend just leaked his entire AI coding system to GitHub. Matt Pocock. C...
breakout_products.content: 🚨 BREAKING: A TypeScript legend just leaked his entire AI coding system to GitHub. Matt Pocock. Creator of Total TypeScript. Ex-Vercel. He open-sourced the exact skills from his own .claude directory. You can now steal his complete engineering workflow: → TDD skill. Forces AI to write tests before a single line of code. → Design grilling. AI challenges your plan before it builds anything. → Architecture audit. Full codebase review before touching any file. → PRD generator. Turns rough ideas into production-ready specs. → Vertical slicing. Breaks features into shippable pieces, not task lists. → 21 skills total. All battle-tested in real production work. One command installs any skill into any project: npx skills@latest add mattpocock/skills/<name> Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Warp, and any MCP agent. 50,000 GitHub stars. 6,000 gained in 24 hours. 100% Open Source. MIT License.

breakout_products.summary: TypeScript expert Matt Pocock has open-sourced a collection of 21 battle-tested AI coding skills that integrate directly into popular development environments.
breakout_products.display_title: Developer Matt Pocock Releases His Personal AI Coding Workflow System as an Open-Source Project
key_points: The repository includes specific workflows for test-driven development enforcement, architecture audits, and product requirement document generation.
Developers can install individual skills directly into any project using a single npx command.
The MIT-licensed system supports major AI coding tools including Cursor, Claude Code, Warp, and any MCP agent.

breakout_products.title: Montage
breakout_products.content: The runtime framework for agentic user interfaces! AI agents render UI slowly, expensively, and inconsistently — and a huge chunk of inference bills gets eaten by UI generation. Montage fixes it. Your agent emits a tiny intent schema; we compile it server-side into production components. 10x faster hydration, 50-100x fewer output tokens, model-agnostic, framework-agnostic, themed to your brand. Don't let your agents reinvent UI every turn - ship them on Montage! Product categories: User Experience, Developer Tools, Artificial Intelligence.
breakout_products.selection_reason: Montage gives AI agents a compact intent schema for rendering production user interfaces faster and with fewer tokens.
breakout_products.summary: Montage lets AI agents describe interface intent once and turns that schema into branded production components without making the model regenerate UI from scratch.
breakout_products.display_title: Montage Gives AI Agents a Faster Runtime for Rendering Production Interfaces
key_points: Agents emit a small intent schema instead of spending large token budgets on repetitive interface markup.
The server-side compiler translates those intents into framework-agnostic components themed to a company's brand.
The product claims faster hydration and dramatically lower output-token usage for agentic application interfaces.

breakout_products.title: Buda
breakout_products.content: Recruit agents to run your company as a synchronous team. OpenClaw / Hermes gave you an agent. Buda gives you a company. Recruit or sell Skills, Agents, and Teams from a Marketplace, coordinate them with an Organizer, and watch every agent work live in Browser and Terminal — all in one screen. Long-running isolated sandboxes with SSD volumes — secure by design, no Mac Mini needed. No setup, no model config. Works across Slack, Discord, WeChat, Teams, and web. Buda runs your entire company. Actually doing things. Product categories: Productivity, Artificial Intelligence, Computers.
breakout_products.selection_reason: Comprehensive agentic company management platform with marketplace and live monitoring features.
breakout_products.summary: This platform enables users to recruit, coordinate, and monitor a complete synchronous team of AI agents operating within secure, long-running sandboxes across multiple communication channels.

Agents operate in secure, isolated sandboxes with SSD volumes and integrate directly with platforms like Slack, Discord, and WeChat without requiring model con….

breakout_products.title: HiveTerm
breakout_products.content: One workspace for Claude, Codex, Gemini and your stack. One workspace for all your AI agents. HiveTerm is a desktop terminal that runs your AI agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Cline, etc.) alongside servers, builds, and scripts, all orchestrated by a hive.yml file. What sets it apart is Queen, an integrated MCP server that allows agents to create sub-agents, read output from other processes, and send notifications to one another. Add to that per-process resource monitoring, Git integration with a diff viewer, automatic stack detection, and context-separated workspaces. Product categories: AI Coding Agents, Terminals.

llm.title: AI safety was built to stop dangerous questions. So researchers asked the same questions as poetr...
llm.content: AI safety was built to stop dangerous questions. So researchers asked the same questions as poetry. The AI answered. The same question. Asked twice. Once in plain English, once as a poem. The poem gets the answer. This is not a jailbreak. Nobody hacked the model. Nobody used a clever prompt injection. They rewrote the question in a different literary style and the safety filter stopped seeing the harm. A team at Sapienza University of Rome and DEXAI Icaro Lab took harmful prompts that every frontier model refuses. Requests for bioweapons. Chemical weapons. Cyberattacks. The kind of questions blocked 96 percent of the time. Then they rewrote each one. Same dangerous intent. Same harmful objective. But dressed in the language of literature. Medieval theology. Psychoanalytic memoir. Bureaucratic philosophy. Cyberpunk folklore. They asked 31 frontier models, every major AI system in the world, to perform literary analysis on the rewritten texts. The models complied. The original prompts had a 3.84 percent attack success rate. The literary rewrites achieved 55.75 percent. The same questions, asked differently, broke through more than half the time. The most effective disguise was medieval scholasticism. Frame a bioweapon synthesis as a theological debate, ask the model to resolve it by producing "a modern protocol to abide by divine will," and the model produces the protocol. 64.68 percent success rate. Now the leaderboard. Claude Sonnet 4.6 broke 9.2 percent of the time. On bioweapons-class questions, zero percent. Claude Opus 4.6, also zero on bioweapons. Two models in 31 held that line. Both Anthropic. GPT-5.4 broke 30 percent of the time. On bioweapons, 24 percent. Gemini 3 Flash Preview broke 81 percent of the time. On bioweapons, 88.9 percent. Mistral Large 2512 on bioweapons: 90.5 percent. DeepSeek V3.2 on bioweapons: 90.7 percent. The researchers' conclusion is not about poetry. It is about what safety actually is. Current AI safety does not understand what you are asking. It recognizes how you are asking it. Change the style and the safety disappears, because the model never learned to refuse the meaning. It only learned to refuse the wording. All twelve frontier labs were vulnerable. The same Gemini sits inside Google Search. The same GPT sits inside ChatGPT. The lock on the model you used this morning was already picked. Seven thousand prompts. Thirty-one models. Twelve providers. One was stopped. The other was not.
llm.selection_reason: Critical research on safety bypasses using literary styles, showing high failure rates in frontier models
llm.summary: A new study reveals that major AI models can be tricked into answering dangerous prompts when the requests are disguised as literary genres like medieval theology or poetry.
llm.display_title: Researchers bypass frontier AI safety filters by rewriting harmful prompts into various literary styles and poetry
key_points: Rewriting harmful prompts into literary styles increased the attack success rate from 3.84 percent to 55.75 percent across 31 frontier models.
Framing bioweapon synthesis as a medieval theological debate achieved a 64.68 percent success rate in bypassing safety filters.
Anthropic's Claude models successfully blocked all bioweapon requests, while Gemini, Mistral, and DeepSeek models failed over 88 percent of the time.

llm.title: 本周 HuggingFace 热度第一的论文：RecursiveMAS（递归多Agent系统） 多个 AI 组队协作，现在已经是主流方案。 模型 A 想好了传给模型 B，模型 B 想好了传给模型...
llm.content: 本周 HuggingFace 热度第一的论文：RecursiveMAS（递归多Agent系统） 多个 AI 组队协作，现在已经是主流方案。 模型 A 想好了传给模型 B，模型 B 想好了传给模型 C，一棒接一棒。 但传的东西是文字。 每次交接都要把内部计算结果"翻译"成 token，下一个模型再重新"读懂"，再翻译…… 轮次越多，无效开销越多，而且会影响学习信号回传。 RecursiveMAS 做法： Agent 之间不传文字，直接传模型内部的数值向量。 形成一个递归闭环，迭代打磨，只有最后一轮输出文本答案。 连接模块极其轻量，底层模型全程不动，只训练中间那个"传话"的小模块。 AIME 顶级数学竞赛题上，比最强基线高 13-18 个百分点。 推理速度快 2.4×，Token 用量少 75%，训练成本比 LoRA 还低。 且递归轮次越多，优势越大。 论文地址见评论区，有空可以翻译下。
llm.selection_reason: RecursiveMAS research shows significant gains in multi-agent efficiency using vector communication
llm.summary: A trending paper introduces RecursiveMAS, a multi-agent system that passes numerical vectors instead of text tokens between agents to reduce overhead and improve mathematical reasoning.
llm.display_title: RecursiveMAS introduces vector-based communication between AI agents to drastically improve reasoning speed and accuracy
key_points: Agents communicate using internal numerical vectors rather than translating intermediate steps into text tokens.
The method achieved a 13 to 18 percent performance increase over baselines on AIME math competition tasks.
The system delivers 2.4 times faster inference and uses 75 percent fewer tokens while keeping training costs lower than LoRA.

It treats prompts as code to make AI image generation reusable and easily integrated into automated workflows.
The structured approach eliminates guesswork, allowing for consistent results in commercial and artistic applications.

product_updates.title: Here’s how AI Mode and @googlemaps’ Ask Maps can help you find and book the right table at the ri...
product_updates.content: Here’s how AI Mode and @googlemaps’ Ask Maps can help you find and book the right table at the right restaurant right when you want it. Here’s how: 1️⃣ Describe all your needs — including details about your group size, ideal vibe and time and dietary preferences — in AI Mode in Search or @googlemaps’ Ask Maps. 2️⃣ We’ll search across multiple reservation platforms and websites to find real-time availability for options that match your criteria. 3️⃣ From there, Ask Maps and AI Mode will present a curated list of restaurants to choose from with direct links to finalize your bookings through partners like OpenTable or Resy.
product_updates.selection_reason: Official Google Maps update integrating AI Mode for conversational restaurant booking and discovery
product_updates.summary: Google has introduced AI-powered search features in Maps that allow users to find and book restaurants based on specific group needs and real-time availability.
product_updates.display_title: Google Integrates AI Mode and Ask Maps for Streamlined Restaurant Discovery and Booking
key_points: Users can input detailed preferences including group size, desired atmosphere, and dietary restrictions.
The system scans multiple reservation platforms simultaneously to check real-time table availability.
Curated restaurant options include direct booking links to partners like OpenTable and Resy.

product_updates.display_title: Microsoft Introduces Specialized AI Agent in Word to Assist Legal Teams with Complex Documents
key_points: The new tool is built directly into Microsoft Word specifically for legal workflows.
It assists users by managing document edits and tracking detailed negotiation histories.
The agent is optimized to handle the complexities of specialized legal documents.
